name: Parque Natural de Armañón – circular by the Corcillo trail
name_original: Parque Natural de Armañón – circular por el sendero del Corcillo
region_intro: Hiking around Guriezo offers a diverse landscape for outdoor exploration, characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and the presence of the Armañón Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, including paths that lead to elevated viewpoints and routes alongside water bodies like the El Juncal reservoir. These Guriezo hiking routes provide opportunities to experience the natural environment of Cantabria. Best hiking trails around Guriezo The most popular hiking route is Parque Natural de Armañón – circular by the Corcillo trail, a 8.96 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peak of the Snows from The Bridge, a moderate 8.03 miles (12.9 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain, leading to panoramic views. Local hikers also love the El Juncal Reservoir – Canal El Juncal loop from Guriezo, a 8.65 miles (13.9 km) trail leading through forested areas and alongside the reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Guriezo is defined by natural park trails, elevated peaks, and reservoir paths. The network offers options for easy family friendly walks as well as more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Guriezo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Guriezo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Guriezo?
A: Guriezo offers a selection of 10 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 1 easy trail, 7 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Guriezo?
A: Absolutely. Guriezo has an easy trail, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24639068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de las Nieves – Pico de las Nieves (Cantabria) loop from Castro Lucio&lt;/a&gt;. This route is ideal for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk with family.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Guriezo's hiking trails?
A: The trails around Guriezo feature a diverse landscape, from rolling hills and dense woodlands to paths alongside water bodies like the El Juncal reservoir. Many routes explore the varied terrain of the Armañón Natural Park and lead to elevated viewpoints, offering a true taste of Cantabria's natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Guriezo?
A: Yes, Guriezo offers several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001223672/parque-natural-de-armanon-circular-by-the-corcillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Armañón – circular by the Corcillo trail&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore the natural park's diverse landscapes. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Juncal Reservoir – Canal El Juncal loop from Guriezo&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views along the water.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking in Guriezo?
A: While hiking in Guriezo, you can discover several natural wonders. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt;, a unique natural monument, or exploring the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cullalvera Cave&lt;/a&gt;. For stunning views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4486690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solpico Summit&lt;/a&gt; is a rewarding destination.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Guriezo?
A: The hiking experience in Guriezo is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse natural park trails, the rewarding elevated peaks, and the serene reservoir paths, highlighting the variety and beauty of the routes.

Q: Is Guriezo a good destination for winter hiking?
A: While Guriezo offers year-round hiking opportunities, winter conditions can vary. Higher elevation trails, such as those leading to the Peak of the Snows, may experience snow and ice. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out during the colder months.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several trails in Guriezo lead to elevated points with panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996937755/peak-of-the-snows-from-the-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peak of the Snows from The Bridge&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, features significant elevation gain, culminating in expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Can I spot wildlife on the trails around Guriezo?
A: The dense woodlands and natural park areas around Guriezo provide habitats for various wildlife. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, hikers often have the chance to observe local bird species and other small animals, especially in quieter sections of the trails within Armañón Natural Park.

Q: What is the best season to go hiking in Guriezo?
A: Guriezo is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for exploring. Summer is also popular, though some trails might be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potential snow at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' trails in Guriezo?
A: While the main routes are well-loved, exploring the various paths within the Armañón Natural Park can lead to quieter, less-trafficked sections. The region's extensive network of trails means there are always opportunities to discover a peaceful corner away from the more popular routes, especially if you venture slightly off the main paths.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments after a hike in Guriezo?
A: Guriezo, as a local community, offers options for refreshments. You'll find local establishments where you can enjoy a meal or a drink after your hike. It's always a good idea to check opening times, especially if hiking during off-peak seasons.
tips: Old containment and distribution dam of the old Mongarrido canal

Mongarrido canal used as a bridge to cross the stream.
On this bridge we can enjoy some beautiful waterfalls that pass below thanks to the torrent of water. This bridge, although it is not as high as its predecessor or as high, its surroundings and the waterfalls that surrounded it, have seemed very beautiful to me.
The Mongarrido canal as it passes through this point is used as a bridge to continue the path to the other side of the Arroyo Remendón, with a considerable height and an arch below it.

waypoints.name: Old Containment Dam and Mongarrido Canal
